title: Styling
text: Define thresholds assign different colors value ranges — useful highlighting high/low values:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_films |>     mutate(Characters = lengths(characters)) |>     arrange(episode_id),   xAxis  = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "episode_id", label = "Star Wars Episode")),   yAxis  = list(list(     colorMap = list(       type       = "piecewise",       thresholds = c(15, 20, 25),       colors     = c("#4caf50", "#ff9800", "#f44336", "#9c27b0")     )   )),   series = list(list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters")),   height = 300 )

title: Styling
text: Map value range color gradient — characters colored birth year:
code: ScatterChart(   height  = 350,   dataset = starwars_people |>     filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), !is.na(birth_year), mass < 200) |>     mutate(id = row_number()),   series  = list(list(     datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass", z = "birth_year"),     label = "Characters"   )),   xAxis  = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),   yAxis  = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),   zAxis  = list(list(     colorMap = list(       type  = "continuous",       min   = 0,       max   = 100,       color = c("#d01c8b", "#f1b6da", "#b8e186", "#4dac26")     )   )) )

title: Styling
text: Map categorical values specific colors — top 3 species membership get distinct color:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_species |>     mutate(n = lengths(people)) |>     arrange(desc(n)) |>     head(3),   xAxis  = list(list(     scaleType = "band",     dataKey   = "name",     colorMap  = list(       type   = "ordinal",       values = c("Human", "Droid", "Naboo"),       colors = c("#1976d2", "#ff9800", "#4caf50")     )   )),   series = list(list(dataKey = "n", label = "Members")),   height = 300 )

title: Styling
text: Use sx prop CSS overrides chart elements using MUI CSS class selectors — rounded bars subtle axis line:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_films |>     mutate(Characters = lengths(characters)) |>     arrange(episode_id),   xAxis  = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "episode_id", label = "Star Wars Episode")),   series = list(list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters")),   sx = list(     "& .MuiBarElement-root" = list(rx = 8),     "& .MuiChartsAxis-line" = list(stroke = "#999", strokeWidth = 2)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Styling
text: Customize line mark appearance sx:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_films |>     mutate(       Characters = lengths(characters),       Planets    = lengths(planets)     ) |>     arrange(episode_id),   xAxis  = list(list(scaleType = "point", dataKey = "episode_id", label = "Star Wars Episode")),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters"),     list(dataKey = "Planets",    label = "Planets")   ),   sx = list(     "& .MuiLineElement-root"  = list(strokeWidth = 3, strokeDasharray = "5 5")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Styling
text: Combine innerRadius, paddingAngle, cornerRadius polished donut look:
code: PieChart(   series = list(list(     data = starwars_people |>       count(gender) |>       filter(!is.na(gender)) |>       select(label = gender, value = n),     innerRadius  = 50,     paddingAngle = 3,     cornerRadius = 5   )),   height = 300 )

title: Styling
text: Pass JavaScript function color adapts current theme mode:
code: SparkLineChart(   data   = starwars_films |> arrange(episode_id) |> mutate(n = lengths(characters)) |> pull(n),   height = 100,   color  = JS("(mode) => (mode === 'light' ? '#1976d2' : '#90caf9')"),   area   = TRUE,   showHighlight = TRUE,   showTooltip = TRUE )

title: Styling
text: Combine ThemeProvider muiMaterial charts cohesive dashboard. Define palette colors R variables reference theme chart: Define palette colors R variables (e.g., primaryColor), pass ThemeProvider’s palette chart’s color prop. keeps colors sync—edit primaryColor, updates everywhere automatically. dashboard layout built muiMaterial components (Box, Card, Typography) visual consistency.
code: library(muiMaterial)  muiMaterialPage(   CssBaseline(),   {     # Define theme colors once in R; both ThemeProvider and chart use the same values     primaryColor <- "#9819d2"     secondaryColor <- "#dc004e"      ThemeProvider(       theme = list(         palette = list(           primary = list(main = primaryColor),           secondary = list(main = secondaryColor)         )       ),       Box(         sx = list(p = 3, bgcolor = "background.paper"),         Typography("Star Wars Characters by Episode", variant = "h5", sx = list(mb = 2)),         Card(           CardContent(             BarChart(               dataset = starwars_films |>                 mutate(Characters = lengths(characters)) |>                 arrange(episode_id),               xAxis = list(                 list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "episode_id", label = "Episode")               ),               series = list(                 list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters", color = primaryColor)               ),               height = 300             )           )         )       )     )   } )

title: Styling
text: details styling, visit MUI X Styling documentation.

title: Tooltip
text: Tooltips provide extra data hovering chart elements. article demonstrates tooltip customization based MUI Tooltip documentation.

title: Tooltip
text: Element counts per film — used throughout vignette:
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr) film_counts <- starwars_films |>   mutate(     Characters = lengths(characters),     Planets    = lengths(planets),     Species    = lengths(species)   ) |>   arrange(episode_id)

title: Tooltip
text: Tooltips can triggered two ways: "item" — data single hovered element (default scatter pie) "axis" — data series hovered axis position (default bar line)

title: Tooltip
code: BarChart(   dataset = film_counts,   layout  = "horizontal",   yAxis   = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "title", width = 220)),   series  = list(     list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters"),     list(dataKey = "Planets",    label = "Planets")   ),   slotProps = list(tooltip = list(trigger = "item")),   height = 320 )

title: Tooltip
code: BarChart(   dataset = film_counts,   layout  = "horizontal",   yAxis   = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "title", width = 220)),   series  = list(     list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters"),     list(dataKey = "Planets",    label = "Planets")   ),   slotProps = list(tooltip = list(trigger = "axis")),   height = 320 )

title: Tooltip
text: Set trigger = "none" completely disable tooltip:
code: BarChart(   dataset = film_counts,   layout  = "horizontal",   yAxis   = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "title", width = 220)),   series  = list(     list(dataKey = "Characters", label = "Characters"),     list(dataKey = "Planets",    label = "Planets")   ),   slotProps = list(tooltip = list(trigger = "none")),   height = 320 )

title: Tooltip
text: Use valueFormatter series control text shown tooltip:
code: BarChart(   dataset = film_counts,   layout  = "horizontal",   yAxis   = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "title", width = 220)),   series  = list(     list(       dataKey        = "Characters",       label          = "Characters",       valueFormatter = JS("(v) => `${v} characters`")     ),     list(       dataKey        = "Planets",       label          = "Planets",       valueFormatter = JS("(v) => `${v} planets`")     )   ),   height = 320 )
